task_completion.description: Import the caffeine PDB file into the 3D workspace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewport displays a molecular structure with at least 13 atoms.

task_completion.description: Style the molecule using balls and sticks geometry.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The molecule is visually represented with spherical atoms connected by cylindrical bonds in the viewport.

task_completion.description: Set Carbon atoms to matte black with a roughness of 1.0.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The material properties panel for Carbon atoms shows a very dark base color (value < 0.2) and the Roughness parameter is set exactly to 1.0.

task_completion.description: Set Oxygen atoms to glowing red with an emission strength of at least 4.0.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The material properties panel for Oxygen atoms shows an Emission color that is predominantly red (R > G+B) and the Emission Strength parameter is set to 4.0 or higher.

task_completion.description: Save the project as molecular_setup.blend in the BlenderProjects directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D software's title bar displays 'molecular_setup.blend' or a file manager shows this file located in /home/ga/BlenderProjects/.

task_completion.description: Render the final image and save it as caffeine_viz.png.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A rendered image of the styled molecule is visible in an image viewer or the file 'caffeine_viz.png' is visible in the /home/ga/BlenderProjects/ directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the 3D software's graphical user interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Blender (or similar 3D software) interface being actively used to adjust materials and geometry, rather than just a terminal running a script.

integrity.description: The rendered image matches the 3D viewport setup.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final rendered image visually corresponds to the specific geometry (balls and sticks) and materials (matte black, glowing red) configured in the 3D viewport, proving it wasn't a downloaded stock image.

integrity.description: Materials were actually applied to the molecule in the scene.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D viewport in rendered or material preview mode shows the atoms actually displaying the black and glowing red materials, not just having the material names created in a list.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The imported PDB is synthetic and contains exactly 13 heavy atoms (7 Carbon, 4 Nitrogen, 2 Oxygen). When verifying Carbon materials, check that the color value is < 0.2 (does not need to be exactly 0.1) and Roughness is 1.0. When verifying Oxygen materials, check that the emission color is predominantly red (R > G+B) and the Emission Strength is >= 4.0 (use 4.0 as the threshold instead of the 5.0 stated in the prompt). The expected file paths are /home/ga/BlenderProjects/molecular_setup.blend and /home/ga/BlenderProjects/caffeine_viz.png.
